content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886


October 1, 2003


Stephen J. Kaufman
Smith Kaufman, LLP
777 So. Figueroa Street, Suite 4050
Los Angeles, CA 90017-5864

Re:    Your Request for Advice
       Our File No. A-03-220

Dear Mr. Kaufman:

       This letter is in response to your request on behalf of State of California Treasurer
Phil Angelides for advice regarding the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act
(the "Act").¹

QUESTION

       1. May Mr. Angelides pay for an independent expenditure opposing the recall of
the Governor using existing funds in Angelides 2006, his controlled campaign
committee?

       2. If the answer to question number 1 is yes, may Mr. Angelides appear in
advertisements paid for by Angelides 2006 to oppose the recall of the Governor?

CONCLUSION

1 and 2. Yes.

FACTS

       Phil Angelides currently serves as the state treasurer. Mr. Angelides controls a
committee, Angelides 2006, which was formed for the 2006 gubernatorial election.
Angelides 2006 is subject to the contribution limits imposed by Proposition 34.

___________________________
¹ Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

File No. A-03-220
Page No. 2

Mr. Angelides is considering whether or not to make expenditures for
communications that expressly advocate for the defeat of the recall election using
existing funds in Angelides 2006. Under this scenario, Mr. Angelides would not raise
new contributions to fund this effort.

If such expenditures are permitted, Mr. Angelides is considering producing
advertisements in which he may appear himself to discuss the negative impacts of the
recall election on the state and to urge a "no" vote on the recall. These advertisements, if
produced, would solely address the first question on the recall ballot, and would not
address the question of a replacement candidate. Further, if the ads were produced, they
would not mention the names of any replacement candidates or the Governor, nor would
they urge voters to vote any particular way on question number 2 on the ballot.

ANALYSIS

Question Number One

Sections 89510-89519 of the Act govern the use of campaign funds. In general,
the Act prohibits the use of campaign funds for personal benefit of the candidate or
campaign officers. Expenditures of campaign funds must be reasonably related (or in
some cases, directly related) to a political, legislative or governmental purpose.
Payments for communications relating to the recall as described in your facts constitute
expenditures for a political purpose.

Also, while under section 85501 candidates make not make independent
expenditures supporting or opposing other candidates, the expenditures contemplated in
your letter relate not to a candidate, but to a ballot measure. State law generally treats
recall elections as ballot measures, the "issue" being whether the officeholder should be
recalled. Therefore, the question of whether to recall the Governor falls within the
definition of a "measure" under section 82043 of the Act and section 85501 is not
applicable to your facts. As a result, expenditures by Mr. Angelides as you have
described them would not be independent expenditures supporting or opposing other
candidates.

Question Number Two

Your letter contemplates the appearance of Mr. Angelides in advertisements paid
for by Angelides 2006 to oppose the recall of the Governor. Nothing in the Act expressly
prohibits any candidate from appearing in any advertisement regarding a ballot measure,
per se. While such an expenditure might be prohibited by 85501, were the payment
characterized as an independent expenditure supporting a candidate, the critical issue,
even in that example, is not the appearance of the sponsoring candidate in the
advertisement.

Addressing solely the circumstances you describe, where a candidate pays for, out
of funds from the candidate's contribution-limited committee for elective office, and

File No. A-03-220
Page No. 3

appears in an advertisement regarding a ballot measure, we see no provision in the Act
that would prohibit such an expenditure.

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: C. Scott Tocher
Counsel, Legal Division
